title: Fix broken logo image in navbar (asset loading issue)

requirements.text: Audit the logo image reference in `frontend/src/components/Header.tsx`: verify the `src` attribute path exactly matches the actual filename on disk (including case sensitivity), confirm the asset exists under `frontend/public/` or the correct static assets directory, and ensure the path used is compatible with the production build output (e.g., using a root-relative `/` prefix or correct Vite asset import rather than a relative path that breaks in nested routes).

quotes: Verify the logo image path in production.
Confirm the logo asset is included in the production build.

acceptanceCriteria: The logo `` tag in `Header.tsx` references a path that resolves correctly in the production build.
The logo filename on disk matches the reference exactly, including letter case.
No broken image icon appears in the navbar in any route (home, listings, detail pages, etc.).
The logo loads correctly when tested in an incognito browser window with cache cleared.

requirements.text: Ensure the logo asset file is physically present in `frontend/public/` (or the appropriate static assets folder) and is included in the production build output. If the file is missing, add or restore it. If it was previously imported via a JS/TS import rather than placed in `public/`, standardise the approach so it is reliably bundled.

quotes: Ensure the file exists on the server.
Confirm the logo asset is included in the production build.

acceptanceCriteria: The logo image file exists in the project at the path referenced by `Header.tsx`.
The asset appears in the production build output directory.
The navbar displays the logo image (not a broken image placeholder) after a fresh production build.

requirements.text: Also verify and fix the logo image reference in `frontend/src/components/Footer.tsx` using the same corrected path/import approach applied to the Header, so the logo is consistent and working across the entire site.

quotes: Right now the header does not look premium because the logo is missing.

acceptanceCriteria: The Footer also renders the logo image without a broken image icon.
Both Header and Footer use the same consistent logo asset path.

constraints: Do not change the backend schema or any Motoko files.
Do not modify any files under frontend/src/hooks/ or frontend/src/components/ui (immutable paths).
Fix must be scoped to asset path resolution and file presence only — do not alter logo sizing or alignment styles established in prior versions.
nonGoals: Changes to navbar layout, spacing, or alignment.
Adding new logo variants or formats.
